title: Roasted Mexican Beef Stir-fry
description: A rich and creamy Polish dish featuring roasted beef with a touch of cilantro for an evening craving.
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, chili powder, and salt. Mix well until evenly coated.
Rub the spice mixture onto both sides of the beef tenderloin and place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 63°C (145°F) using an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the meat.
Remove from heat and let it rest for about 5 minutes before slicing against the grain.
While beef rests, sauté red bell pepper in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat until softened, about 8-10 minutes.
Add sliced beef to the skillet with peppers. Stir-fry for another 3-4 minutes or until heated through.
In a small bowl, mix sour cream and chopped cilantro leaves together until smooth. Serve stir-fried beef over cooked rice or as is, topped with the sour cream mixture.
If desired, sprinkle a bit of creamy corn on top before serving.
